channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Canada's next prime minister has managed the financial crisis, Brexit and now Trump's trade war
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Canada’s next prime minister has already helped run two Group of Seven economies in crisis and now will try to steer Canada through a looming trade war brought by U.S. President Donald Trump, a threat of annexation and an expected federal election.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Court-martial convenes for Pentagon leaker already facing years behind bars
item.description: BEDFORD, Mass. (AP) — A court-martial convented on Monday for Massachusetts Air National Guard member Jack Teixeira, who was sentenced in federal court last year to 15 years in prison for leaking highly classified military documents after the most consequential national security breach in years.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: President Trump defends tariff policy
item.description: President Donald Trump continues to defend tariffs as a way to address unfair trade and illegal drugs coming across the border, but critics warn they'll cause higher prices for Americans.
item.dc:creator: Maddie Biertempfel, WGN 9

item.title: DC to begin reconstructing Black Lives Matter Plaza
item.description: Crews from the District Department of Transportation will begin working on what is currently the Black Lives Matter Plaza in Washington, D.C. on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Syria announces the end of a military operation against Assad-linked gunmen in a coastal region
item.description: DAMASCUS, Syria (AP) — Syria's interim government on Monday announced the end of a days-long military operation against insurgents loyal to ousted president Bashar Assad and his family in the worst fighting since the end of the 13-year civil war in December.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Republicans worry Trump's tariffs could harm economy
item.description: Republican lawmakers are growing alarmed over signs that President Trump’s expanding trade war is hurting the economy, something they’re hearing from constituents at home who are struggling to adapt to Trump’s zigzagging tariff pronouncements.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pope following Vatican's spiritual retreat from afar as he recovers in hospital
item.description: On Thursday, the Vatican will mark the 12th anniversary of Francis’ election, the first with the pope out of sight but still very much in charge.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Drawing huge crowds, Bernie Sanders steps into leadership of the anti-Trump resistance
item.description: WARREN, Mich. (AP) — Bernie Sanders is standing alone on the back of a pickup truck shouting into a bullhorn. He's facing several hundred ecstatic voters huddled outside a suburban Detroit high school — the group that did not fit inside the high school's gym or two overflow rooms.
item.dc:creator: STEVE PEOPLES, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: An Arizona prisoner whose execution is coming up isn't asking for a reprieve
item.description: A prisoner scheduled to be executed in what would be Arizona’s first use of the death penalty in over two years will not ask for a reprieve from his death sentence.
item.dc:creator: Jacques Billeaud, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Scholars stranded in America and abroad amid funding freeze of State Department programs
item.description: Fulbright scholar Aubrey Lay was supposed to get paid for three months of work by the U.S. government through his teaching assistantship at a school for Ukrainian refugees in Estonia. Instead, he only got about one week's pay and no word on when he might see the rest of his grant.
item.dc:creator: OLIVIA DIAZ and HEATHER HOLLINGSWORTH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Air Force intercepts aircraft flying in a restricted zone near Mar-a-Lago
item.description: Air Force fighter jets on Sunday intercepted a civilian aircraft flying in the temporarily restricted airspace near President Donald Trump’s Florida home, bringing the number of violations to more than 20 since Trump took office on Jan. 20.
item.dc:creator: Zeke Miller, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Top US health agency makes $25,000 buyout offer to most of its 80,000 employees
item.description: The mass email went out to a “broad population of HHS employees," landing in their inboxes days before agency heads are due to offer plans for shrinking their workforces.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Myles Garrett becomes the highest-paid non-quarterback in NFL history at $40 million per year
item.description: Myles Garrett is staying in Cleveland after the Browns gave him a record four-year contract extension that makes the four-time All-Pro edge rusher the highest-paid non-quarterback in NFL history.
item.dc:creator: ROB MAADDI, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Why these 2 states didn't 'spring forward' to daylight saving time this morning
item.description: You may have been groggy this morning thanks to our clocks “springing forward” an hour overnight. Unless, of course, you live in either of the two states that don’t observe daylight saving time.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Suzanne Simpson murder: Texas attorneys sift through evidence as husband’s case rocks affluent neighborhood
item.description: Texas real estate agent Suzanne Simpson vanished in a wealthy area of San Antonio in October 2024, and the case is progressing even though authorities have not found her body.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Bannon: DOGE 'waste, fraud, abuse’ cuts not enough to curb inflation
item.description: Bannon, Trump’s former adviser and critic of Musk, has condemned the tech giant's motives and methods.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, ABC 4

item.title: 8 hurt when driver plows vehicle into Southern California car dealership
item.description: INGLEWOOD, Calif. (AP) — Eight people were injured Saturday when a driver plowed a vehicle into a CarMax location in the Los Angeles area.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Suspects in LA murder of good Samaritan who tried to stop catalytic converter theft were in country illegally
item.description: Two suspects arrested for allegedly murdering a Los Angeles man were in the country illegally and have lengthy criminal records, police sources said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Why Chi-Chi’s restaurants are returning 2 decades after their closure
item.description: While multiple casual dining chains are looking to reduce their footprints this year, a once-beloved but long-shuttered brand is preparing for a comeback.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, ABC 4

item.title: Ohio couple charged after hundreds of living and dead rats found inside home
item.description: A Stark County couple is facing animal cruelty charges after authorities discovered horrific conditions inside their home, which included hundreds of living and dead rats.
item.dc:creator: Jack Shea, KRON4

item.title: Hawks, butterflies and a song for her murdered son help Georgia mom reclaim joy after killings
item.description: ROSWELL, Ga. (AP) — A hawk swooped in and landed on a tree, then watched as Michele and Jeremy Davis mourned their murdered son at a vigil outside his high school.
item.dc:creator: JEFF MARTIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: National auto parts retailer closing all California stores
item.description: Advance Auto Parts plans to close all remaining locations in California by mid-March as the automotive giant undergoes a massive restructuring plan.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, NewsNation

item.title: A total lunar eclipse and blood moon will be visible in March: How and when to watch
item.description: A treat awaits sky gazers in the month of March – the moon will take on a deep, reddish hue as it slips into the Earth's shadow during a total lunar eclipse.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, Fox 5

item.title: Pickleball isn't named after a dog: How the sport really earned its moniker
item.description: You may have heard a rumor about the game being named after a dog. Spoiler alert: That isn't the case.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, WGN 9

item.title: Video shows mom, teen fight 8th grader on Indianapolis school bus
item.description: Indianapolis police are investigating after two unauthorized family members boarded a middle school bus and physically fought with a student.
item.dc:creator: Joe Schroeder, NewsNation

item.title: Watch the moon turn red during a total lunar eclipse in March
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A total lunar eclipse will flush the moon red Thursday night into Friday morning across the Western Hemisphere. The best views will be from North America and South America. Parts of Africa and Europe may catch a glimpse.
item.dc:creator: ADITHI RAMAKRISHNAN, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Alabama minor is jailed with adults pre-trial in violation of federal law
item.description: MONTGOMERY, Ala. (AP) — Djovani Jean-Pierre spent his 17th birthday in a small county jail in rural northern Alabama, where he has languished for more than half the school year awaiting trial. On the advice of his 51-year-old cellmate, Jean-Pierre avoids contact with others in the jail.
item.dc:creator: SAFIYAH RIDDLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Violent and sudden. What a firing squad execution looked like through my eyes
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — I've now watched through glass and bars as 11 men were put to death at a South Carolina prison. None of the previous 10 prepared me for watching the firing squad death of Brad Sigmon on Friday night. I might now be unique among U.S.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Expert tells judge to drop charges against NYC Mayor Eric Adams without letting them be refiled
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A judge has no choice but to grant the Justice Department’s unusual and divisive request to dismiss New York City Mayor Eric Adams’ corruption case, a court-appointed lawyer said Friday.
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Late car payments hit highest level in decades
item.description: Americans are falling behind on their monthly car payments at the highest rate in more than 30 years.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, ABC 4

item.title: A car pulled from a river may tell what happened to an Oregon family of 5 that went missing in 1958
item.description: After two days of dredging, a crane on Friday pulled from the Columbia River a Ford station wagon that officials believe belonged to an Oregon family who disappeared while on a trip 66 years ago to find Christmas greenery.
item.dc:creator: MARTHA BELLISLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Reopening Texas detention centers to hold deportees worries migrant advocates
item.description: Two controversial immigration detention facilities in South Texas are being reopened for the detention and deportation of migrant women and families, officials tell Border Report.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, Fox 5

item.title: Federal judge won't order immigration officials to change school arrests policy
item.description: DENVER (AP) — Immigration authorities don't need to revert to a Biden-era policy limiting arrests at schools after officials in Denver challenged new policies from the Trump administration, a federal judge ruled Friday. U.S.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN SLEVIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: State Dept using AI to ban visas over perceived pro-Hamas posts
item.description: The initiative, started under Secretary of State Marco Rubio, will use AI to scan student visa holders’ social media accounts.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, Fox 5

item.title: Gene Hackman, Betsy Arakawa causes of death announced
item.description: The causes of death for actor Gene Hackman and his wife Betsy Arakawa were released Friday after days of mystery and speculation.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, ABC 4

item.title: Tony! Toni! Tone! guitarist D'Wayne Wiggins dies
item.description: D'Wayne Wiggins, guitarist and founding member of Tony! Toni! Tone!, has died, according to a social media post from his family. The guitarist, who was a native of Oakland, had been battling bladder cancer.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, KRON4

item.title: Cockpit recorder likely hadn't worked 'for several years' before jet crashed in Philadelphia: NTSB
item.description: The cockpit voice recorder in the medical transport jet that slammed into a Philadelphia neighborhood in January had likely not worked for years, according to a preliminary report released by the National Transportation Safety Board (NTSB) Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, WGN 9
